heading: Plans for performance partnerships
label: 300x–59 Plans for performance partnerships
notes: Codification, referred to in subsec. (b), was in the original “the date of the enactment of this Act”, which was translated as meaning the date of enactment of , which amended this section generally, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.October 17, 2000Pub. L. 106–310

Amendments2022—Subsecs. (a)(2). substituted “substance use disorders” for “substance abuse” in two places.Pub. L. 117–328Subsecs. (d). substituted “substance use disorders” for “substance abuse”.Pub. L. 117–3282000— amended section catchline and text generally. Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and to the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate, a report on the activities of the States carried out pursuant to the programs established in sections 300x and 300x–21 of this title. Such report may include any recommendations of the Secretary for appropriate changes in legislation.”Pub. L. 106–310January 24, 1994

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “Not later than , the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and to the Committee on Labor and Human Resources of the Senate, a report on the activities of the States carried out pursuant to the programs established in sections 300x and 300x–21 of this title. Such report may include any recommendations of the Secretary for appropriate changes in legislation.”Pub. L. 106–310January 24, 1994

children.text: a description of the flexibility that would be given to the States under the plan;

the common set of performance measures that would be used for accountability, including measures that would be used for the program under subpart II for pregnant addicts, HIV transmission, tuberculosis, and those with a co-occurring substance use disorders and mental disorders, and for programs under subpart I for children with serious emotional disturbance and adults with serious mental illness and for individuals with co-occurring mental health and substance use disorders disorders;1So in original.1
the definitions for the data elements to be used under the plan;
the obstacles to implementation of the plan and the manner in which such obstacles would be resolved;
the resources needed to implement the performance partnerships under the plan; and
an implementation strategy complete with recommendations for any necessary legislation.

children.heading: Submission
children.label: b Submission
children.text: Not later than 2 years after , the plans developed under subsection (a) shall be submitted to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate and the Committee on Commerce of the House of Representatives.October 17, 2000

children.heading: Information
children.label: c Information
children.text: As the elements of the plans described in subsection (a) are developed, States are encouraged to provide information to the Secretary on a voluntary basis.

children.heading: Participants
children.label: d Participants
children.text: The Secretary shall include among those interested groups that participate in the development of the plan consumers of mental health or substance use disorders services, providers, representatives of political divisions of States, and representatives of racial and ethnic groups including Native Americans.
